description: seventh month (April), ark comes to rest in Ararat
first month (September), Noah removes part of roof
Jehovah promises land of Canaan to Abraham and his offspring
Abraham visits Egypt; Pharaoh takes Sarah, returns her; Abraham returns to Canaan
Sarah (age 75) gives Hagar to Abraham (age 85) to have a son
covenant of circumcision made with Abraham (age 99)
Abimelech, king of Gerar, tries to take Sarah (age 89) for harem
Isaac and Rebekah in Gerar, Abimelech protects both
Leah bears Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Judah
Jacob wrestles angel, is named Israel
Jacob and Esau meet, make peace
Joseph (age 28) interprets dreams of baker and cupbearer
Joseph (age 30) becomes prime minister of Egypt
Nisan 14 (Abib) (March-April), Moses (age 80) leads Exodus from Egypt
Nisan, Israelites cross Red Sea, sing victory song, Miriam leads women
Iyyar (Ziv) (April-May), Israelites reach wilderness of Sin, quail provided, manna begins forming
Sivan (May-June), Law Covenant with Israel at Sinai
ark of the covenant inaugurated with tabernacle and priesthood
Ab 1 (July-August), Aaron dies at Mount Hor (age 123)
census of Israel on Plains of Moab
Shebat (January-February), covenant with Israel at Moab
Nisan 15, Israelites begin eating of the land’s produce
beginning of time period of judges and saviors of Israel
Saul anointed by Samuel, begins reign
David (age 30) becomes king of Judah at Hebron
David (age 37) becomes king over all Israel
David (age 38+) brings ark of covenant to Jerusalem
ark of the covenant moved into temple
Sivan (May-June), Asa holds assembly in Jerusalem to make covenant
Zimri becomes king of Israel for seven days
Omri and Tibni become rival kings of Israel
Ahaziah coregent with father Ahab as king of Israel
Ahaziah becomes sole king of Israel
Jehoram (son of Ahab) becomes king of Israel
Jehoram (son of Jehoshaphat) (age 32) reigns with father over Judah
Jehoram (son of Jehoshaphat) becomes sole ruler of Judah
Ahaziah (age 22) (grandson of Jehoshaphat; grandson of Ahab through Athaliah) becomes king of Judah
Jehu anointed as king of Israel
Jehoram (son of Ahab) killed by Jehu
Ahaziah (grandson of Jehoshaphat) killed by Jehu
Jehu’s first year as king of Israel
Jehoash (son of Ahaziah) (age 7) becomes king of Judah
Jehoash (son of Jehoahaz) becomes king of Israel
Jeroboam II becomes king of Israel
Jeroboam II begins last regnal year
Shallum becomes king of Israel for one month
Menahem’s first regnal year as king of Israel
Syria and Israel war against Judah
Tiglath-pileser of Assyria exiles portions of Israel
Hezekiah’s (age 25) first regnal year as king of Judah
ten-tribe kingdom of Israel falls to Assyria
Manasseh’s (age 12) first regnal year as king of Judah
book of the Law found during temple repairs
Josiah returns ark of the covenant to temple
Josiah dies in battle with Pharaoh Necho(h)
Nebuchadnezzar defeats Pharaoh Necho(h) at battle of Carchemish
Baruch reads scroll in temple courtyard
Jehoiachin (age 18) becomes king of Judah for 3 months, is exiled
Daniel, Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah exiled to Babylon
Ezekiel enacts siege and destruction of Jerusalem
Ezekiel’s vision of false worship in temple
Tebeth 10 (December-January), final siege of Jerusalem begins
Ezekiel’s messages against enemy nations begin
Jeremiah taken into custody by King Zedekiah
ark of covenant removed from temple
Ezekiel focuses on restoration of pure worship
Nebuchadnezzar exiles more Jews, conquers Moab and Ammon, and invades Egypt
Ezekiel foretells Nebuchadnezzar’s invasion of Egypt
claim of being year that Jerusalem was destroyed by Babylonians
Jehoiachin released from prison in Babylon
Cyrus the Great becomes king of Persia
Heshvan 3 (October-November) Cyrus enters Babylon as king
Darius the Mede made king of Babylon, coregent with or followed by Cyrus
Cyrus decrees release of Jews, temple rebuilding
Nisan 24 (March-April), Daniel receives prophecy of kings of north and south
Cambyses II coregent with Cyrus; Cyrus the Great dies
Artaxerxes (Ezr 4:7) (Smerdis [Bardiya or Gaumata]) usurps Persian throne
Darius I’s first regnal year as king of Babylon
Xerxes I (Ahasuerus) coregent with father Darius I (Hystaspis)
Nisan 13 (March-April), Xerxes I (Ahasuerus) through Haman decrees extermination of Jews
Sivan 23 (May-June), Xerxes I (Ahasuerus) through Mordecai decrees that Jews may defend themselves
Nisan 1 to Ab 1 (four months March/April to June/July), Ezra travels to Jerusalem
Chislev (November-December), Nehemiah visited by Jews from Jerusalem
Nisan (March-April), Artaxerxes Longimanus sends Nehemiah to rebuild Jerusalem and walls
Nehemiah returns to Artaxerxes’ court in Persia
Seleucus I (Nicator) founds Antioch in Syria
Septuagint translation of the Hebrew Scriptures into Greek begun
Macedonia and Greece fall to Rome
effort to burn all Hebrew Scripture scrolls
Rome conquers Achaean League, destroys Corinth
Roman General Pompey inspects Most Holy, ark of the covenant absent
victory of Octavius and Mark Antony on Plain of Philippi
Herod the Great becomes king of Judea
Achaia includes southern and central Greece
Roman province of Asia made senatorial, governed by proconsul
Gabriel foretells birth of John the Baptizer
Jesus presented at temple after 40 days old
Joseph, Mary, Jesus flee to Egypt
Joseph, Mary, Jesus return from Egypt
